nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Defines the 'idea-refine' Claude Code skill: a three-phase (Understand & Expand, Evaluate & Converge, Sharpen & Ship) ideation partner that turns a vague idea into a concrete markdown one-pager with problem statement, recommended direction, assumptions, MVP scope, and a 'not doing' list.
nodes.languageNotes: Claude Code skill markdown with YAML frontmatter (name/description) followed by structured trigger phrases, process phases, anti-patterns, and a verification checklist.

nodes.name: examples.md
nodes.summary: Worked examples illustrating the idea-refine skill's three-phase process across a vague early-stage concept, a feature idea within an existing codebase, and a non-product workflow idea, ending with takeaways on what makes a good ideation session.

nodes.name: frameworks.md
nodes.summary: Reference catalog of ideation frameworks (SCAMPER, How Might We, First Principles, Jobs to Be Done, Constraint-Based Ideation, Pre-mortem, Analogous Inspiration) used to generate idea variations during Phase 1 of the idea-refine skill.

nodes.name: refinement-criteria.md
nodes.summary: Evaluation rubric for stress-testing idea directions during Phase 2 of the idea-refine skill, covering user value, feasibility, and differentiation dimensions, an assumption audit (must/should/might be true), and MVP scoping principles.
